strait.name: Strait of Hormuz
strait.closure_type: De facto (insurance-driven + active attacks on vessels)
strait.closure_method: Asymmetric denial via drone and missile strikes on commercial vessels, triggering insurance-driven shutdown. IRGC used sea drones, anti-ship missiles, and shore-launched projectiles to strike 17+ vessels since Feb 28. War risk insurance withdrawn or repriced at ~5% of vessel value — a 100x increase from pre-war 0.02-0.05%. For a $100M tanker, $5M per transit. Trump ordered DFC to provide $20B in political risk reinsurance (Chubb as lead underwriter), but JPMorgan estimates actual exposure at $352B across 329 tankers. Reports of naval mine deployment began ~March 10; US destroyed 30+ Iranian minelayers. Iran's stockpile estimated at 2,000-6,000 naval mines; 'a few dozen' deployed in Strait by March 13. Iran retains 80%+ of its small boats and minelayers. London Joint War Committee extended high-risk area to include waters around Oman.
strait.status: Functionally blockaded. Only 21 tankers crossed Feb 28 through ~Mar 16, vs 100+ ships/day pre-war. Daily transits in single digits. Half of March transits were shadow fleet (Lloyd's List). Iran selectively allowing passage: India (2 LPG tankers), Pakistan, Turkey, Saudi each transited with individual approval. IRGC officially confirmed closure Mar 2. IMO estimates ~20,000 seafarers stranded; Bloomberg reports 40,000. ~15,000 cruise passengers stranded on 6+ major ships. ~400 vessels in Gulf of Oman backlog. Widespread GPS/AIS disruption: 44 injected signal zones, 92 denial areas. IMO emergency session (Mar 18-19): negotiating non-binding safe maritime corridor resolution for seafarer evacuation. War risk premiums: 0.125% to ~5% of vessel value (40x increase). Strait effectively uninsurable for most commercial operators. VLCC rates hit all-time record $423,736/day. Jones Act waived Mar 18 for 60 days. Seven-nation joint statement (Mar 19) expressing readiness for 'preparatory planning' — analysts called it 'largely a gesture to placate Trump.' UK agreed to expand US base access for defensive strikes on Iranian Hormuz missile sites.

impact.tankers_transiting_current_note: Only 21 tankers have transited since Feb 28 versus pre-war daily average of 138 (UKMTO). MarineTraffic Mar 15-17: 15 vessel transits in 3 days (8 dry bulk, 5 tankers, 2 LPG, ~87% outbound). Pakistan-flagged Aframax first non-Iranian AIS-broadcasting cargo vessel Mar 17. ~50% of Mar 1-8 transits were shadow fleet (AIS disabled). Iran exporting ~1.5 mbd to China uninterrupted.
impact.insurance_cost_note: War-risk premiums surged from 0.02-0.05% to ~5% of vessel value — a 100x increase. For $100M tanker = $5M per transit. Major P&I clubs withdrew Gulf coverage by March 2. Trump: $20B DFC reinsurance (Chubb lead). JPMorgan: $352B actual exposure across 329 tankers. London Joint War Committee extended high-risk area to include Oman waters.

alternative_routes.name: East-West Pipeline (Saudi Petroline)
alternative_routes.status: Full capacity since March 11 per Aramco CEO Nasser. Effective Red Sea export via Yanbu ~4-5 mbd after domestic refinery consumption.

alternative_routes.name: IPSA Pipeline (Iraq-Saudi)
alternative_routes.status: Mothballed since 1990 — would need 2-3 months rehabilitation

alternative_routes.name: Habshan-Fujairah ADCOP (UAE)
alternative_routes.status: Intermittent — Fujairah attacked 4 times since Mar 9. Oil loadings suspended multiple times. Bunkering volumes down 60%+.

alternative_routes.name: Iraq-Kurdistan (Kirkuk-Ceyhan)
alternative_routes.status: Restarted March 18 at ~250,000 bpd. Potential 450,000 bpd. Only 6-13% of Iraq's pre-war Basra exports (3.4 mbd).

impact.shadow_tanker_transit_note: USNI/Lloyd's List: shadow fleet ~50% of all transits Mar 1-8. ~3 transits/day mostly Chinese-flagged and Iranian-permitted vessels. Pakistan-flagged Aframax first non-Iranian AIS vessel Mar 17.
impact.vessels_attacked_note: 20+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28 per UKMTO. Two additional vessels struck March 20. Breakdown: 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%). At least 4+ seafarer fatalities confirmed.
impact.kharg_island_strike_note: US struck 90+ military targets on Kharg Island Day 14-15. Oil infrastructure deliberately spared.
impact.south_pars_strike_note: Israeli Air Force struck South Pars Phases 3/4/5/6 and Asaluyeh hub with US coordination. First upstream energy strike. ~100M cm³/day gas offline. Two refineries halted. Qatar condemned.
impact.iran_selective_passage_note: Iran exporting ~1.5 mbd to China uninterrupted. Selective passage for non-Western vessels on case-by-case basis.
impact.us_escort_status: No operational escort capability. USS Tripoli exited Malacca Strait Mar 18, ETA Mar 24-25. USS Boxer left San Diego Mar 19, ETA early April. Combined: ~4,400 Marines. CRITICAL MCM GAP: USS Tulsa/Santa Barbara in Singapore as of Mar 18 — thousands of miles from Bahrain. Christian Science Monitor: prior Navy assessment found 'unreliable unmanned vehicles, critical single-point failures, and sonar that couldn't see.' UK agreed to expand US base access for defensive strikes. Seven-nation joint statement on Hormuz called 'largely a gesture' by analysts.
impact.irgc_threat_march_18: IRGC issued 'urgent warning' following South Pars strike — named SAMREF Refinery (Saudi), Jubail Petrochemical Complex (Saudi), Al Hosn Gas Field (UAE), Mesaieed (Qatar), Ras Laffan (Qatar) as 'direct and legitimate targets in the coming hours.' Urged civilian/worker evacuation.
impact.ruwais_refinery_status: Shut — Abu Dhabi's largest refinery (922,000 bpd) offline after earlier drone strikes.
impact.day_21_events: Day 21 — Region-wide energy war. Ras Laffan LNG damaged (12.8 MTPA, 3-5yr repair, $20B/yr loss). SAMREF/Yanbu struck. Kuwait refineries hit. UAE: 334 BMs + 1,714 drones cumulative. Bahrain: 139 missiles + 238 drones intercepted. 7,800+ US targets. 4 officials killed. F-35 struck. Boxer deploys. Brent $108.19. Gas $3.91. S&P new 2026 low below 200-day MA. IEA: 'largest supply disruption in history.' Goldman: oil >$100 through 2027.
